item.title: Blackstone Data Center REIT Seeks to Raise $1.75 Billion in IPO
item.description: Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $1.75 billion in a US initial public offering, offering investors the chance to capitalize on a surge in building artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: How US and China’s Allies Struggle to Balance Trade Ties With Both
item.description: Big economies managing ties with the US and China are struggling to withstand shocks from both

item.title: Musk Sketches Apocalyptic Vision in Bid to Block OpenAI’s Transition
item.description: Warnings about various risks to humankind are a typical part of the billionaire’s rhetoric

item.title: US Stock Futures Dip on Reports of Increasing Mideast Tension
item.description: US stock futures saw volatile early Monday trading with news reports of Iranian missiles hitting a US Navy ship. While those reports cited IRGC-aligned Fars News Agency, a senior US official said that a ship was not hit by Iranian missiles, according to Axios, which helped futures erase some of the earlier losses.

item.title: Asia May Be Entering an AI-Fueled Industrial Super Cycle
item.description: Morgan Stanley economists see AI, the energy transition and defense spending fueling a capital expenditure-led expansion for Asia.

item.title: Amex GBT Said to Near Sale to General Catalyst-, Alpha Wave-Backed Long Lake
item.description: A startup backed by General Catalyst Partners is in advanced talks to acquire Global Business Travel Group Inc., the travel platform spun out of American Express Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Geothermal Energy Developer Fervo Seeks $1.33 Billion in US IPO
item.description: Fervo Energy Co., a geothermal energy developer, is looking to raise as much as $1.33 billion in a US initial public offering, as more firms seek to capitalize on the growing power demand for data centers.

item.title: Immune System Biotech Odyssey Seeks $238.3 Million in US IPO
item.description: Odyssey Therapeutics Inc., a clinical stage biotechnology firm focused on autoimmune and inflammatory diseases, is seeking to raise $238.3 million in an initial public offering.

item.title: The Growing Divide of Tech’s Winners and Losers
item.description: Alphabet, Meta shares head in opposite directions after earnings

item.title: KKR-Backed Air Ambulance Firm GMR Seeks $797.9 Million in US IPO
item.description: GMR Solutions Inc., an air and ground emergency medical services company, is seeking to raise as much as $797.9 million in a US initial public offering, joining a growing list of healthcare firms seeking to tap investors.

item.title: Medical Device Maker Mobia Seeks to Raise $160 Million in US IPO
item.description: Mobia Medical Inc., a maker of medical devices to help stroke survivors, is seeking to raise as much as $160 million in a US initial public offering.

item.title: Green Sky Capital Backs Egypt Sustainable Aviation Fuel Facility
item.description: Green Sky Capital signed financing for a sustainable fuel facility in Egypt, joining other regional players in backing the project as airlines look for ways to diversify their energy requirements.

item.title: Nvidia Rival Cerebras Seeks to Raise $3.5 Billion in US IPO
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is seeking to raise as much as $3.5 billion in an initial public offering, as the artificial intelligence chipmaker and data center operator looks to counter rivals in the red-hot sector.

item.title: State Healthcare Sites Are Sharing Personal Data with Big Tech
item.description: Some state healthcare sites sent personal information — location, race, immigration data — to TikTok, Meta and other big tech companies. Experts say existing privacy laws aren’t enough.

item.title: Carlyle Acquires Healthcare RCM Providers Knack and EqualizeRCM
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. has acquired a majority stake in healthcare revenue cycle management firms Knack RCM and EqualizeRCM, it said in a statement Monday, without disclosing terms.

item.title: Henko Weighs Stake Sale in Portuguese Engineering Firm Quadrante
item.description: Henko Partners is exploring the sale of its minority stake in Quadrante, a deal that could value the Portuguese engineering and environmental consultancy at more than €300 million ($352 million), according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Bank of America Didn’t ‘Wall-Cross’ Segantii Before Esprit Block
item.description: Bank of America Corp. traders working on a 2017 block sale in the shares of fashion retailer Esprit Holdings Ltd. didn’t “wall-cross” Segantii Capital Management before disclosing some information about the potential deal, Hong Kong prosecutors said on Monday.

item.title: South Africa Factories Turn Upbeat as War Brings Orders Forward
item.description: A gauge measuring South African manufacturer sentiment rose in April to its best level in two years, driven by business activity and new sales orders, potentially reflecting front-loading ahead of price increases due to the Iran war.

item.title: Colombian Presidential Candidate Seeks Trump’s Help for War on Cocaine Gangs
item.description: Colombians face a hard, familiar choice in this month’s presidential election: negotiate with violent criminals, or go back to war against them.

item.title: Poland’s PZU Buys MetLife Ukraine in Postwar Reconstruction Bet
item.description: Poland’s biggest insurance company PZU SA agreed to buy MetLife Inc.’s Ukrainian unit, marking a significant expansion into the war-torn market as Warsaw seeks a leading role in the country’s reconstruction.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Sees Tech Earnings Eclipsing Iran War for Stocks
item.description: Strong US corporate earnings led by a buoyant tech sector are overshadowing fears that the Middle East conflict could weigh on stocks, according to strategists at Morgan Stanley.

item.title: ECB Rate Hike in June Is ‘All but Inevitable,’ Kazimir Says
item.description: It’s highly likely that the European Central Bank will have to raise interest rates at its next meeting in June, Governing Council member Peter Kazimir said.

item.title: ECB’s Villeroy Urges Caution and Readiness on Rate Hikes
item.description: The European Central Bank must be both cautious and ready to act on interest rates should inflation spread beyond a surge in oil prices, outgoing Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de Galhau said.

item.title: Hazelview Adds Presima Assets in Push to Scale REIT Business
item.description: Hazelview Investments Inc., a private money manager that specializes in commercial real estate, is combining its listed real estate business with that of Presima Securities ULC in a move to bulk up its global platform.

item.title: World’s Super-Rich Boost Conflict Economy Wagers Amid Iran War
item.description: Members of the world’s ultra-wealthy are striking bets on a range of sectors seeing valuation swings in the fallout from the war in the Middle East, signaling how rising geopolitical tensions are reshaping private investors’ focus.

item.title: Trump Announces Plan to Get Ships Out of Hormuz, Vows US Support
item.description: President Donald Trump said the US will help ships stranded in the Persian Gulf transit the Strait of Hormuz, describing it as a “humanitarian gesture” and testing his ability to restore traffic through the strategic waterway for the first time since the war with Iran began.

item.title: How Baltimore's Mayor Is Fighting the City's Vacant Housing Crisis
item.description: Brandon Scott on re-energizing the city.

item.title: Euro-Zone Inflation Jump Seen as Temporary, ECB Survey Shows
item.description: Euro-zone inflation will jump to 2.7% on average this year but return close to the European Central Bank’s 2% target already next year, according to the institution’s quarterly survey of professional forecasters.

item.title: Odd Lots: How Baltimore Is Fighting Vacant Housing (Podcast)
item.description: Since Mayor Brandon Scott took office in 2020, he has fixated on a very visible problem in Baltimore: the tens of thousands of vacant homes that dot the city. It’s hard to build new houses when there are so many that sit empty and unused. And the process of tracking down owners, convincing them to sell their vacant properties, and then converting those homes into usable housing supply is a tall task. But in the last few years, the number of vacant homes in Baltimore has dropped from 16,000 to ju

item.title: India Industry Hub Set for Shock Poll Results as Film Star Leads
item.description: Tamil Nadu, one of India’s most industrialized states, is headed for a surprise election outcome, with a film star pulling well ahead of the incumbent in the ongoing vote counting.
